item.title: Timmy, Germany's humpback whale, likely lived for only 5 days after controversial rescue effort
item.description: The humpback whale that kept Germany spellbound for months likely lived for roughly five days after the final controversial rescue attempt failed to guide it back to its natural habitat in the Atlantic Ocean, officials said Friday. The whale, nicknamed “Timmy” and “Hope” by German media, was found dead on May 14, stranded just off the small island of Anholt in the Kattegat, the broad strait between Denmark and Sweden that connects the Baltic Sea to the North Sea. The discovery of the body ended months of a spectacular and contentious rescue effort that culminated May 2, when the mammal was transported toward the North Sea in a barge in the final rescue attempt. Scientists, government officials, the public and a private initiative sparred over whether it was more humane to let the weakened and sick animal die on its own or continue the rescue efforts.

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike multiple targets on Crimea
item.description: Ukrainian drones have struck several targets on the Russian-annexed Crimean Peninsula, sparking a large fire at a power plant near Simferopol, the pro-Ukrainian Telegram channel Krymsky Veter reported on Friday. Anti-aircraft fire was reported at the Russian Saki air base, it added, while explosions were heard in other parts of the peninsula.

item.title: Kenya holds a memorial service for 16 victims of last month's girls school fire
item.description: Hundreds of mourners gathered on Friday in Kenya’s central town of Gilgil for a memorial service to honor the lives of 16 students who died in a fire at a girls school last month that police said was caused by arson. The remains of the girls, who were students at Utumishi Girls Academy, were placed in white coffins adorned with flowers and topped with their portraits. The nine accused girls, who were students at the school, remain in police custody, with interrogations revealing that the May 28 blaze was started by lighting a mattress at the dormitory’s exit using a matchstick and paraffin.

item.title: 2 collisions on a Hungarian highway kill 8 people
item.description: Two collisions one after another on a highway in western Hungary early Friday killed eight people, police said. A truck caught fire after colliding with a construction vehicle near the city of Győr around 4.30 a.m., killing one person and snarling traffic, police said. About half an hour later, a minibus with Moldovan license plates slammed into a truck that stopped on the highway following the first accident.

The significant progress the world was making against HIV had already been stalling, but "it is now in peril because of sudden cuts," sh

item.title: Funding cuts drive sharp drop in HIV prevention, UNAIDS says
item.description: LONDON, June 12 (Reuters) - Almost 40% fewer people got a drug to help prevent HIV infection in 2025 than in the previous year, as global aid funding cuts ‌hit preventive services particularly hard, early data presented by UNAIDS on Friday showed. Across 62 countries, 38% ‌fewer people received pre-exposure prophylaxis, or PrEP, at least once in 2025 compared to 2024, the agency said.

item.title: Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths when powerful quake hit the southern Philippines
item.description: Philippine officials said Friday that years of disaster-preparedness drills helped prevent a larger casualty toll when one of the strongest earthquakes in 50 years struck the south and left 55 people dead with 31 others missing. The 7.8 magnitude offshore quake, which struck Monday off Sarangani province, injured about 1,120 people and displaced more than 45,000 people, about half them still in emergency shelters, after the quake damaged more than 12,600 houses across farming towns and cities. Days after the earthquake hit, more videos of the chaotic moments have been posted on social media showing horrified crowds witnessing the collapse of small buildings, and flag-raising ceremonies turning chaotic when the ground started to shake on the first day of school after a long summer break.

item.title: The migration pact: What's in the EU's landmark asylum reform?
item.description: A major reform of European migration rules aimed at hardening border procedures and overhauling the asylum process comes into force on Friday. Here is an overview of the main changes:   - Border procedures -   Migrants irregularly entering the European Union will undergo identity and security checks in a process lasting up to seven days.

The craft came into his life only after he lost his sight during one of Ukraine’s bloodiest and longest battles.

item.title: AI image, unrelated building collapse photo falsely linked to deadly Philippine tremor
item.description: After a powerful earthquake hit the southern Philippines in June 2026, two images were shared in posts claiming they showed an unfinished building flattened by the quake and construction workers at the site who died. Authorities told AFP that no such incident involving a large group of construction workers had occurred in the recent tremor. The image of the workers is in fact AI-generated, and the photo of the collapsed building was taken two weeks before the quake in the country’s north.

The princess, affectionately known as Bha in Thailand, died peacefully at King Chulalongkorn Memorial Hospital in Bangkok on Thursday evening, the royal household said.

item.description: A 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of southern Philippines in June 2026, toppling buildings and prompting a shopping mall to close for safety inspections, but a video shared online that appears to show shoppers scrambling for safety does not depict the recent disaster. The footage was filmed more than two years ago during another earthquake in Mindanao and AFP also distributed a similar video in November 2023.

item.description: South Korea’s ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense minister were sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday in a case alleging Yoon ordered drone flights over Pyongyang in 2024 to heighten tensions with North Korea and justify declaring martial law at home. The Seoul Central District Court found Yoon and his ex-defense minister, Kim Yong Hyun, guilty of aiding an adversary and abusing their power, saying they sought to provoke North Korea into launching armed attacks or other serious provocations against South Korea to manufacture a national emergency.

Bajrakitiyabha was active in justice reform efforts and best known for her Kamlangjai, or “Inspire,” project to help rehabilitate incarcerated Thai women ahead of their release. Bajrakitiyabha was hospitalized in December 2022 after falling unconscious while training dogs for an army exhibition.

item.title: Report: Six dead and several injured in Israeli attacks on Lebanon
item.description: At least six people have been killed in fresh Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon, the Lebanese state news agency NNA reported on Thursday. It said four people were killed in an attack on the southern town of Abbāssiyyah, with two additional deaths following Israeli attacks on the coastal city of Tyre and in Baalbek in the east.

item.title: German parliament extends Bundeswehr missions in the Balkans
item.description: The German Armed Forces will continue to participate in two international missions in the Balkans for another year, following a German parliamentary decision on Thursday evening. The Bundestag agreed to extend the mandates for the NATO mission KFOR in Kosovo and the EU mission EUFOR Althea in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

item.title: Russian officials say three killed in Ukrainian attacks

A Ukrainian drone hit a bus in the village of Voznesenovka near the border, the crisis staff of the Belgorod region said, adding that a woman was killed and 11 others were injured.

item.title: UN experts, MSF condemn crackdown on women by Afghan morality police

The partnership will focus on cooperation in areas such as data, ​connectivity, cybersecurity, and the protection of minors, Virkkunen, European Commission's executive vice-president for tech sovereignty, security and democracy told reporters on the sidelines of the Web Summit in Rio. item.title: Iran's Kharg Island is key to its oil exports. Targeting it carries major risks
item.description: Iran's Kharg Island, home to a terminal through which the country exports most of its oil, has emerged as a focus of the war launched in late February by the United States and Israel. Strikes on oil infrastructure on Kharg — or a ground invasion — would severely curb Iran's oil exports, a key source of revenue for the Islamic Republic. An assault would also mark a major escalation that could provoke even heavier retaliatory attacks on Gulf Arab infrastructure and further drive up oil prices.

item.title: Brazil reports drop in Amazon deforestation rates, pushing back on US tariff accusations
item.description: Brazilian officials on Thursday announced a sharp drop in deforestation rates, pushing back on one of the arguments that the Trump administration used last week to justify additional tariffs on the South American country. In May, Amazon deforestation was 61.4% lower than in the same month in 2025, according to officials from the National Institute for Space Research, or INPE, and the Ministry of Environment. Deforestation over the same period fell 12% in the Cerrado, a savanna in central Brazil that has long been under pressure from the powerful agribusiness sector.

item.title: New PM military secretary and Washington attaché join IDF General Staff
item.description: The IDF promoted Guy Markizeno and Tal Politis to the General Staff, placing them in two of Israel's most influential security positions amid ongoing regional challenges. item.title: Sudanese paramilitary drone strikes kill at least 15 people in central region, officials say
item.description: Sudanese paramilitary forces carried out drone strikes overnight in central Sudan, killing at least 15 people and wounding dozens, health officials said Thursday, as the use of unmanned aircraft becomes increasingly common in the more than three-year war in the African country. The attacks, which started late Wednesday, targeted various areas of the city of el-Obeid, including near an army position, according to two health officials at el-Obed Hospital, which received the victims. The war in Sudan erupted in April 2023, after long-simmering tensions between the army and paramilitary Rapid Support Forces, or RSF.

item.title: Northern Ireland's former unionist leader tells jury he did not sexually abuse two children

At the opening of his trial at Newry Crown Court two weeks ago, Jeffrey Donaldson, 63, pleaded not guilty to one count of rape, four counts of gross indecency and 13 indecent assault charges involving two alleged victims from 1985 to 2008. Both the alleged victims have given evidence at the trial that they were abused as children.

item.title: Poland to jail online streamers of violent crime, rape, cruelty for up to 5 years
item.description: Polish lawmakers voted on Thursday to crack down on so-called 'trash streaming', ‌imposing jail terms of up to five ‌years for online broadcasts of crimes like rape and murder as ​well as cruelty to animals and violence aimed at humiliating people. The move is part of a broader push by Poland to tighten regulation of online content. ‌Recent measures include ⁠banning the use of mobile phones by children under 16 in schools and ⁠introducing stricter age verification rules to access pornography. Under the new provisions, broadcasting crimes punishable by more than ​five years ​in prison, including murder ​or rape, will itself ‌be classed as a separate offence punishable by up to five years behind bars.

item.title: Courthouse glimpse of prominent Gaza doctor renews calls for his release from Israeli detention

Fourteen doctors from Gaza are still being held in Israeli prisons, PHRI said.

item.title: IDF 91st Division destroys anti-tank launcher, seizes weapons, kills Hezbollah terrorists
item.description: According to the statement, the IDF also located and seized Kalashnikov rifles, shotguns, and other small arms, as well as RPGs and rockets in a raid on a Hezbollah weapons storage facility. IDF soldiers destroyed an anti-tank missile launcher belonging to Hezbollah terrorists in southern Lebanon on Wednesday, the IDF confirmed.

item.title: After 144 Years, Pope Leo XIV marks the completion of Sagrada Familia's tallest tower in Barcelona
item.description: The pontiff blessed the new Tower of Jesus Christ at the Sagrada Família in a ceremony attended by around 120,000 people, ending with a light and fireworks show.View on euronews

item.title: 5 police officers shot dead in Mexico as World Cup kicks off
item.description: Five Mexican police officers were killed and five others wounded on the eve of the World Cup opener in Mexico City, authorities said.

item.title: Bus to nowhere? City launches 'Line Zero' on the streets
item.description: In the town of Baden, passengers can now use a bus service that runs without a fixed route or timetable. An invitation to switch off and slow down?View on euronews

item.title: Estonian lawmakers back deal to rent out prison cells to Sweden
item.description: The Estonian parliament has given the green light to an agreement with Sweden that foresees the transfer of up to 600 Swedish prisoners to Estonian cells, as Sweden struggles to accommodate inmates amid a rise in gang-related crime. Lawmakers on Wednesday also backed a number of legal reforms that are necessary for the implementation of the deal and still need to be approved by President Alar Karis.

item.title: Sardinian beach bans umbrellas for anyone between 10 and 65 years old
item.description: Local authorities say the new rules are in place to protect the environment

item.title: A cracked coffin, a funeral and the hunt for Ebola's patient zero
item.description: June 11 (Reuters) - By the time the truck pulled away from the morgue in Bunia, the morning heat hung heavily over the road to Mongbwalu. The body of a 44-year-old Congolese pastor lay inside a wooden coffin strapped into the back of an aging Nissan SUV with the backseats pushed flat.

item.title: Pope arrives in Spain's Canary Islands to meet migrants
item.description: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Spain's Canary Islands on Thursday to meet migrants, many of whom survived a dangerous Atlantic crossing to reach the archipelago.Leo is due to visit the port of Arguineguin on the island of Gran Canaria, where he will meet migrants and lay a floral offering in honour of those who died during attempted crossings.

item.title: Ukrainian forces under pressure in Donetsk city of Kostiantynivka
item.description: The situation for Ukrainian soldiers defending the city of Kostiantynivka from Russian forces in eastern Ukraine has deteriorated further, according to the news portal Hromadske. "Kostiantynivka itself is in a state of semi-encirclement, as the enemy has advanced in the area of Chasiv Yar and Berestok," Serhii Yaryi, commander of a drone battalion, said in an interview with Hromadske on Thursday.

item.title: NATO commander highlights critical role of data in modern combat
item.description: BERLIN, June 11 (Reuters) - NATO's top military commander on Thursday highlighted the importance of battlefield data processing in modern warfare, pointing to conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East.

item.title: Residents repeatedly woken by Waymo test gone wrong
item.description: US firm Waymo apologises after a car kept getting stuck up a cul-de-sac letting off sirens at night.

item.title: Fire reported on tanker off Oman
item.description: A fire has broken out on a tanker off Oman, the UK's Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said on Thursday, citing local authorities. The incident was recorded north-east of the port city of Sohar.

item.title: Ukraine's drone hunters are evolving. The next generation is getting faster.
item.description: The companies behind Ukraine's top popular interceptor drones are making their products faster to keep pace with Russia's new jet-powered Shaheds.

item.title: A mass funeral is held for 22 Pakistani soldiers who died in a helicopter crash in Kashmir
item.description: Rescuers recovered the remains of all 22 soldiers aboard a military helicopter that crashed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir the previous day, officials said Thursday, confirming there were no survivors, as senior government and military officials attended a mass funeral for the victims. The helicopter crashed Wednesday in Muzaffarabad, the regional capital, apparently because of a technical fault, according to Pakistan’s military. An Associated Press reporter counted 22 coffins draped in Pakistan’s national flag at a funeral ceremony.

item.title: Ukrainian drones target Moscow again, mayor says, as Kyiv claims oil refinery strike
item.description: Russia's Defense Ministry said it downed 330 Ukrainian drones overnight.

item.title: Albania's Adriatic Coast Development Linked to Jared Kushner Sparks Nightly Protests
item.description: Albania – A proposed luxury resort spanning an abandoned island and a stretch of protected coastline has become the focus of sustained public demonstrations in the southern part of the country. The project, tied to an investment firm connected with Jared Kushner, has drawn thousands of participants who gather each evening to voice concerns over

item.title: Jordan says it intercepted 20 missiles as US, Iran trade fresh attacks
item.description: Jordanian air defences intercepted 20 missiles launched from Iran overnight, state media reported on Thursday, after the US and Iran exchanged fresh attacks. Debris came down during the operation, but it did not result in casualties or damage, state news agency Petra reported, citing the military.

item.title: Iran warns Mideast truce 'practically meaningless' after US strikes
item.description: Iran warned on Thursday that the shaky ceasefire in the three-month Middle East war was now "practically meaningless" following fresh strikes by the United States that saw Tehran respond with attacks around the region.Iran's foreign ministry said on Thursday that "the illegal and criminal attacks perpetrated by the United States in recent hours not only constitute a flagrant violation... but also render the ceasefire practically meaningless".

item.title: Video shows protest in Indonesia, not Muslims clashing with officers in France
item.description: Months-old clips of Indonesian protesters skirmishing with police has resurfaced in posts falsely claiming it shows Muslims attacking French police. The clips circulated after violent clashes between youths and police in French cities on the night of May 30, but previously circulated months earlier during protests in Indonesia initially sparked by discontent over economic inequality.

item.title: Nearly 118 million people were displaced by conflict and persecution last year, UN says
item.description: The U.N. refugee agency said forced displacement of people due to conflict or persecution fell in 2025 for the first time in a decade. The total number of people forcibly displaced by conflict, violence or persecution at the end of 2025 was 117.8 million. The figure includes refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced and other groups in need of international protection.

item.title: Moose put down after wandering into central Oslo
item.description: Norwegian police said Thursday that a moose that had wandered into downtown Oslo, drawing curious crowds, had been shot and killed."For animal welfare reasons, the moose was put down" by the wildlife authorities, the police said.

item.title: IRGC general threatens to 'turn entire region into hell' if Strait of Hormuz destabilized
item.description: "Will you make the sacred Strait of Hormuz unsafe? We will turn the entire region into hell for you from across Iran,” IRGC Aerospace Force chief, Brig. -Gen.

item.title: Russia unblocks Roblox after widespread child anger
item.description: Russia has lifted its ban on the popular gaming platform Roblox, after tens of thousands of children and parents sent letters complaining about the measure.The platform -- which allows users to build their own games and share them with others -- was among Russia's most popular mobile games, tying third with TikTok in usage time among children in early 2025, according to Kaspersky Lab, a Moscow-based global cybersecurity firm.

item.title: India says 3 killed after US military enforces Iran oil blockade, fires on tanker
item.description: Trump said Wednesday that Iran would "pay the price" if negotiations failed.

item.title: Three crew found dead after US attacks tanker off Oman, India says
item.description: Three missing crew members have been found dead after the US military attacked a tanker in the Gulf of Oman, Indian officials said on Thursday. "Sadly, three Indian seafarers initially reported missing are now confirmed dead after bodies have been located and identified," Indian Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal said on X.

item.title: Germany committed to peacetime Strait of Hormuz mission, says Merz
item.description: Germany remains ready to participate in a mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz once the conflict between the United States and Iran is over, Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on Thursday. Berlin's commitment is valid as long as the necessary conditions are fulfilled, Merz told lawmakers in the Bundestag, Germany's lower house of parliament.

item.title: Factbox-Inside the cockpit: How Air India's Boeing Dreamliner flight ended in disaster
item.description: June 11 () - Indian investigators are expected to delay issuing a final report into a deadly Air India Boeing 787 crash by the one-year anniversary on Friday, citing the need ‌to complete an analysis of the plane's engines, according to a source with knowledge of the ‌matter. Here is the sequence of events - by the seconds - on June 12, 2025, as detailed by Indian investigators in their released on July ​12 last year: 05:47 GMT (11:17 a.

item.title: Factbox-Who were the two pilots who flew the Air India jet that crashed?
item.description: June 11 () - Indian investigators are expected to delay issuing a final report into a deadly Air India Boeing 787 crash by the one-year anniversary on Friday, citing the ‌need to complete an analysis of the plane's engines, according to a source with knowledge ‌of the matter. A cockpit recording of dialogue between the two pilots supported the view that the captain cut the flow of fuel to the plane's engines, ​according to U.

item.title: Ousted Turkish opposition leader's team resigns to trigger chairman vote
item.description: ANKARA, June 11 (Reuters) - The team of the ousted leader of Turkey's main opposition, Ozgur Ozel, has resigned from the party's assembly, his office said on Thursday, adding the move should legally

item.title: Clip of Israeli soldiers destroying Lebanon church is AI-generated
item.description: Weeks after an Israeli soldier was caught on camera destroying a Jesus statue in Lebanon, a video surfaced on social media of two soldiers appearing to brag about vandalising a church in the southern town of Debl. But local Lebanese authorities told AFP the town does not have a church resembling the one in the circulating video, and an analysis of the footage found signs it was AI-generated.

item.title: UK, Australia, Canada launch $4m peace fund for Israel-Palestine conflict
item.description: Fund will provide humanitarian support to Palestinians, crack down on Israeli settlers and support Gaza peace plan.

item.title: Merz pledges ongoing German support for Ukraine in Bundestag address
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has pledged Berlin's continued support for Ukraine against Russia in a major address to the Bundestag, Germany's lower house of parliament. The goal remains a just and lasting peace "that also takes our security interests into account," Merz said on Thursday.

item.title: Robotaxis are coming to Europe — and the EU wants to speed things up
item.description: France, Germany, Italy and 14 other countries have signed a joint declaration to coordinate autonomous vehicle testing across borders, paving the way for faster adoption of the technology across Europe.View on euronews

item.title: Thailand condemns Chinese Uyghurs to death for 2015 shrine bombing
item.description: Two Chinese Uyghur men were sentenced to death Thursday for carrying out a 2015 attack on a Bangkok shrine that killed 20 people, a long-awaited verdict in Thailand's deadliest bombing case.The court therefore imposed the harshest penalty available under the law, the death sentence," one member of the four-judge panel said Thursday as the lengthy verdict was read out.

item.title: Thai court sentences 2 Uyghur men to death over 2015 Bangkok bombing that killed 20
item.description: A court in Thailand on Thursday convicted and sentenced to death two members of China’s Muslim Uyghur minority over a 2015 bombing at a Bangkok landmark that killed 20 people and injured more than 120. Yusufu Mieraili and Bilal Mohammad were arrested shortly after the Aug. 17, 2015, bombing of the Erawan shrine, which is a popular tourist destination, particularly for visitors from China.

item.title: Taiwan says its sovereignty cannot be 'violated', as China ends coast guard patrol
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Thai court sentences two men to death over Bangkok shrine bombing
item.description: Twenty people died and 120 others were injured in the explosion at the Erawan shrine 11 years ago.

item.title: Pope Leo heads to Canary Islands on mission to help migrants
item.description: ABOARD THE PAPAL FLIGHT, June 11 (Reuters) - Pope Leo set off on Thursday for the final leg of his visit to ‌Spain, flying from Barcelona to the Canary Islands, where he will meet ‌migrants who have braved dangerous Atlantic waters as he urges the world to treat immigrants more ​humanely. The stop in the Spanish archipelago off the western coast of Africa is a centrepiece of the pope's week-long tour of Spain, in which he has also warned that escalating conflicts have pushed the world into a pr

item.title: Solomon Islands says China security pact to remain secret
item.description: The contents of a secretive security pact between China and the Solomon Islands will remain undisclosed due to legal restrictions, the Pacific nation's new government told AFP on Thursday.His office confirmed to AFP on Thursday that the deal would have to remain secret.

item.title: Scotland's papers: Calls for calm after disorder and Tartan Army jets off
item.description: A review of the front page stories from the daily newspapers in Scotland.

item.title: Clashes erupt as police use water cannon near Belfast
item.description: Police used water cannon during a second night of unrest in Northern Ireland after a Belfast stabbing sparked anti-migrant tensions.

item.title: Spain: Pope Leo XIV blesses new Sagrada Familia tower, now world’s tallest church
item.description: Pope Leo XIV blesses Sagrada Familia’s new tower in Barcelona, now the world’s tallest church, in a mass marking 100 years since Antoni Gaudí’s death.

item.title: Family of British toddler criticises police as Australian inquiry into cold case murders begins
item.description: NSW inquiry will also examine whether notorious serial killer Ivan Milat was responsible for more murders.

item.title: Europe Today: EU Sports Commissioner on Euronews as biggest-ever World Cup kicks off
item.description: The much anticipated FIFA World Cup kicks off today, bringing together some of the world's biggest footballers for a month of matches. We speak exclusively to Glenn Micallef, the EU Commissioner for Sports to hear the Brussels view on one of the most political World Cups to date.

item.title: Pope visiting 'dock of shame' in Canary Islands where migrants slept in squalor
item.description: Pope Leo XIV is visiting the Canary Islands on Thursday to draw attention to the plight of migrants who risk their lives every year trying to reach Europe, fulfilling a wish of Pope Francis to visit one of the epicenters of the European migration debate. Leo is spending the final two days of his weeklong trip to Spain in the Canary Islands, a Spanish archipelago closer to Africa than the Iberian Peninsula that is a key point of entry for migrants smuggled from West Africa. Most poignantly, he will commemorate the thousands of lives lost at sea from a port that in 2020 became known as the “dock of shame” because of the squalid conditions migrants lived in when they came ashore during a spike in arrivals.

item.title: British Magazine Perfectly Skewers Donald Trump's Ego With New World Cup Cover
item.description: Private Eye put words into the president's mouth on its soccer-themed front page.

item.title: 'Victory' or 'peace': Russian Orthodox believers question Church's war stance
item.description: Inside a majestic domed church at one of Russia's oldest monasteries, bearded priests in red-and-gold frocks recite prayers calling for "victory" in the four-year war against Ukraine.Priests who refused to recite the victory liturgy have faced sanctions and some anti-war clergy left Russia.

item.title: Ukrainian mother's agony highlights abuse and weaponisation of draft
item.description: The last time 68-year-old Tetiana Zaitseva saw her son Artem, she was bringing him clothes after he was detained and taken to an army recruitment centre the night before.Police and soldiers detained Artem during the night between May 4 and 5, 2024, in the Ukrainian city of Kryvyi Rig, and brought him to a territorial recruitment centre.

item.title: Air India crash survivor: Tell me why my brother died
item.description: The sole survivor of the Air India plane crash that killed 241 people has called for answers over his brother’s death.

item.title: What to know about the stabbing that set off fiery riots in Northern Ireland
item.description: The suspect, a 30-year-old Sudanese man who had claimed asylum in the U.K., appeared Wednesday in Belfast Magistrates’ Court on charges of attempted murder, threatening to kill a second person and carrying a knife. The court appearance came between two nights of fiery violence that first erupted Tuesday when masked men set fire to several homes they believed to house immigrants, torched a Belfast bus and pelted police with rocks and other objects. Hadi Alodid used a kitchen knife to blind Stephen Ogilvie in the left eye and carved deep wounds on his head, face and back, police said.

item.title: Iran war day 104: Iran attacks US bases, closes strait after Trump strikes
item.description: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, launches retaliatory strikes on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan after US attacks.

item.title: Views sought on future plans for Alderney's waters
item.description: The States wants to create the island's first Marine Spatial Plan with the help of the public.

item.title: Chinese police detain man after dog torture videos spark outrage
item.description: The man allegedly posed as an "adopter" of animals but ended up abusing the dogs and cats he took in.

item.title: 'Wonderful' letters by Wilberforce to be auctioned
item.description: The letters offer "fascinating insights" into the Hull MP who fought for the abolition of slavery.

item.title: Foreign workers say they were paid less than $2 an hour to build a new US Consulate in Milan
item.description: Foreign workers building a sprawling $350 million American Consulate in Milan were paid less than $2 an hour after being promised fair wages, according to Associated Press interviews with five former employees and a review of their employment letters and pay stubs. Italian prosecutors are investigating Montgomery, Alabama-based Caddell Construction, a major builder of U.S. diplomatic missions. Two of its managers in Italy were arrested this month on suspicion of labor exploitation, one while boarding a flight to leave the country and another planning to flee, prosecutors said.

item.title: Ukraine is marking an annual 'special day' to celebrate drone warfare, Zelenskyy says
item.description: Zelenskyy said Ukraine's dedicated drone warfare branch had inflicted $40 billion worth of damage to Russia in a single year of operation.

item.title: NYT Analysis Suggests U.S. Strikes Hit Drinking Water Facility In Iran
item.description: The publication noted that it was unclear if the U.S. military intentionally struck the facility or knew what it was.

item.title: Yaba, the ‘crazy pill’ pushing thousands into addiction
item.description: In the back room of a messy flat somewhere in the dizzying sprawl of Dhaka, a young man tears off a strip of aluminium foil and places on it a tangerine-coloured pill.

item.title: Fact Check: Photo Of Wounded Face Is NOT Of Belfast Attack Victim Steven Ogilvie -- Police Confirm
item.description: Does a viral image circulating on the internet show Belfast knife attack victim Steven Ogilvie with bloody slash marks and stitches on his face in the hospital? No, that's not true: Belfast police confirmed to Lead Stories that the man...

item.title: Old video misrepresented as scene from India migrant crackdown
item.description: India's ruling government in May 2026 ordered a migrant crackdown in West Bengal state that borders Bangladesh, sparking an exodus to the frontier, but footage of a confrontation involving people armed with sticks and long knives is unrelated. Posts falsely claimed the video depicts migrants attacking Indian border troops, but it was actually taken in January 2025 during a clash between Indian and Bangladeshi farmers.

item.title: Philippine town seeks immediate airlift of food to ease hunger in quake-hit villages
item.description: The mayor of a southern Philippine town that was devastated by a powerful earthquake pleaded Thursday for helicopters to transport food to stave off hunger in several landslide-isolated villages. The 7.8 magnitude offshore quake, one of the strongest to hit the Philippine archipelago in a half century, struck Monday off the southern province of Sarangani and has left at least 47 people dead and injured 688 with 31 still missing. Sarangani reported 20 dead from the quake, the highest toll from the affected provinces, mostly due to a landslide that buried houses in the coastal town of Glan, according to the government's Office of Civil Defense, which deals with major disasters.

item.title: One in 70 people worldwide is forcibly displaced: UNHCR
item.description: At least 117.8 million people worldwide remain forcibly displaced due to conflict, violence, and persecution.

item.title: Technology Becomes Most Targeted Industry as China-Nexus Adversaries Hunt AI
item.description: CrowdStrike says China-nexus hackers hit tech firms hardest to steal AI capabilities and close its innovation gap.

item.title: Pope Leo celebrates mass at Gaudí's Sagrada Família in Barcelona
item.description: Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday celebrated a mass in Barcelona marking 100 years since the death of architect Antoni Gaudí in the still-unfinished Sagrada Família basilica, attended by around 4,000 people including Spain's King Felipe VI, Queen Letizia and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. The pontiff had earlier prayed at Gaudí's tomb in the basilica crypt before leading the service in the landmark church, which has been under construction for 144 years.

item.title: The Indian workers training AI robots to take their jobs
item.description: With a smartphone strapped to her head, Indian housewife Nagireddy Sriramyachandra films herself slicing mangoes to train AI-powered robots to take on household jobs in the future."I may get a robot myself in the future," she added.

item.title: Iran’s media posts photos purportedly showing US bomb remains at destroyed water reservoir
item.description: Iran’s semi-official news agency posted photos of a drinking water reservoir that officials said was destroyed in a missile strike as well as images of munition fragments that experts told CNN appeared to be from a US-made bomb.

item.title: Is China's Xi worried that North Korea wants "to confront the U.S."?
item.description: A veteran North Korea analyst says Xi Jinping's visit to North Korea was about keeping tabs on an emboldened Kim Jong Un who wants "to confront the U.S."

item.title: In ageing South Korea, AI dolls care for the elderly
item.description: In her tiny apartment in South Korea, where she lives alone, 78-year-old Bang Chun-ja spends her days with a childlike AI-powered doll she says she prefers to people.In her apartment, another user, 79-year-old Kim Young-bun, said the doll was a source of comfort.

item.title: After 25 days of local pressure, residents stopped a highway that threatened mudflats used by a bird with fewer than 500 left
item.description: Wetlands also help buffer coastlines, support biodiversity, and sustain natural systems that benefit nearby communities.

item.title: After 25 days of local pressure, residents stopped a highway that threatened mudflats used by a bird with fewer than 500 left
item.description: Wetlands also help buffer coastlines, support biodiversity, and sustain natural systems that benefit nearby communities.

item.title: Trump lauds Armenia PM's 'decisive' election win in face of Russian pressure
item.description: June 11 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump congratulated Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on Wednesday for his Civil Contract party's 'decisive victory' in weekend elections.

item.title: The Latest: Iran and US exchange fire for second day as hostiles escalate in Mideast region
item.description: Iran retaliated against U.S. air and cruise missile strikes as hostilities escalated Thursday with attacks against Kuwait and Bahrain in renewed fighting after U.S. President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations. Bahrain is the headquarters of the U.S. Navy's 5th Fleet. The new assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war appeared to have stalled, with Iran insisting it would maintain its grip on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Severely injured hiker survives night in Austrian Alps
item.description: A severely injured German hiker was rescued in the Tyrolean Alps after surviving a night in snow at around 2,200 metres following a large-scale search operation, Austrian police said on Wednesday. The man, from the south-western German state of Baden-Württemberg, had set out alone on a hike near Bach close to the Bavarian border on Tuesday morning before falling in mountainous terrain, police said.

item.title: EU top court opinion due on Covid-19 vaccine transparency appeal
item.description: The European Commission's alleged lack of transparency in purchasing Covid-19 vaccines at the height of the pandemic is back in the spotlight on Thursday when the EU's highest court is set to communicate on two related cases. In 2024, the General Court of the European Union, the bloc's second-highest court, already ruled that the commission had failed to give the public sufficient access to the purchase agreements for vaccines.

item.title: UK, Australia and Canada launch fund for peace in Israel and Palestine
item.description: Britain, Australia and Canada will launch a £3 million ($4 million) fund on Thursday to help support peace in Israel and Palestine. The initiative, dubbed the "International Peace Fund," is intended to tackle the root causes of the Israel-Palestine conflict and pave the way towards a two-state solution.

item.title: Fact Check: Video Of Fires Does NOT Show Belfast
item.description: Does a viral video of fires capture the aftermath of the June 9, 2026, anti-immigration protests in Belfast, Northern Ireland? No, that's not true: The recording showed explosions that same day on a gas pipeline in Russia's Dagestan. The original...

item.title: Russia's conscripts recount pressure to fight in Ukraine
item.description: After Russian police started using facial-recognition cameras to identify men wanted by military authorities, a young bank worker spent weeks avoiding the Moscow metro. In Moscow, facial-recognition cameras and a unified recruitment system have made men easier to find and faster to process.

item.title: Pilots demand answers ahead of Air India crash anniversary
item.description: India's aviation accident probe agency is facing renewed criticism from pilot groups ahead of the anniversary of the 2025 Air India Boeing-787 crash in Ahmedabad, which killed 260 people.- 'Path toward answers' - On Friday, relatives of the victims will meet in Ahmedabad at a conference organised by lawyers, along with aviation and air safety experts, to "discuss the path toward answers and safer skies".

item.title: Iran says it attacked US air bases in Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Iran's armed forces said they had attacked targets in Bahrain and Kuwait in response to US strikes in southern Iran. In Bahrain, a regional headquarters of the US Navy was attacked with so-called kamikaze drones, according to a statement by the military carried by state media.

item.title: Hundreds of police deployed in Mexico City ahead of World Cup opening
item.description: Hundreds of police were deployed to Mexico City's International Airport ahead of the World Cup opening, as authorities stepped up security measures amid the threat of teacher protests on the evening of the football tournament's kick-off. Officers equipped with helmets, shields and batons were stationed inside and outside the Benito Juárez airport terminal on Wednesday, according to videos carried by local media.

item.title: Maasai women turn drought into income through fodder farming in Tanzania
item.description: As drought kills livestock, Maasai women are turning drought-resistant grass into animal feed and income.

item.title: Thousands march in Pakistan-administered Kashmir as clashes kill at least 15
item.description: An uneasy silence has blanketed the capital, with empty streets, closed shops and patrolling police.

item.title: Norwegian court denies request from ailing crown princess’ son
item.description: A Norwegian court has denied a request from Marius Borg Høiby, son of Crown Princess Mette-Merit, to leave custody because of his mother's illness.

item.title: Macron says trust in France institutions 'at stake' after girl's killing
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday said he feared for trust in France's institutions after a botched investigation into the main suspect in an 11-year-old girl's likely murder triggered public outrage."It is trust in our institutions that is at stake," Macron told a cabinet meeting, according to government spokeswoman Maud Bregeon.

item.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz closed after US strikes
item.description: Iran early on Thursday said it was fully closing the Strait of Hormuz and banning all vessel traffic through the strategic waterway after the US military said it launched fresh strikes on multiple targets in the country on the orders of President Donald Trump. Iran's military said the move was prompted by a changed security situation following recent US attacks, according to a statement carried by state media.

item.title: Canada Announces Bill Banning Social Media For Anyone Under 16
item.description: The regulation also imposes new safety expectations on 'AI chatbot services.'

item.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz closed after US strikes
item.description: Iran's military said it is fully closing the Strait of Hormuz following recent US strikes on targets in the south of the country, according to a statement carried by state media. The military headquarters of the Islamic Republic said the move was prompted by a changed security situation following recent US attacks.

item.title: Canada proposes teen social media ban - with workaround for tech firms
item.description: The bill includes sweeping measures to curtail "harmful content" online and would create a regulator.

item.description: The final conclusions of the investigation have yet to be published, although more could become apparent in the coming days.

item.title: Canada seeks to ban social media accounts for children under 16, joining growing global effort
item.description: Canada introduced legislation on Wednesday that could bar children younger than 16 from having social media accounts unless the companies show they can make their platforms safe. Canada is joining a growing global effort to tighten safety protections. Canadian government officials said social media platforms can obtain an exemption if they have put in place sufficient safeguards.

item.title: Bernie Sanders On Albania Protests Over Jared Kushner Project: 'This Is What Global Oligarchy Looks Like'
item.description: On Monday, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said large-scale protests in Albania over a planned luxury resort project involving Jared Kushner reflect rising opposition to "global oligarchy." Albania Protests Against Kushner-Led Resort In a post on X, Sanders pointed to demonstrations...

item.title: Saudi Arabia lifts 5-year ban on Lebanese imports, marking a thaw in Gulf-Lebanon ties
item.description: Saudi Arabia announced Wednesday the end of a ban it imposed on Lebanese imports five years ago, marking a major step in attempts to rebuild relations between Lebanon and Gulf countries. In one notable case, Saudi Arabia announced it had seized over 5 million pills of the amphetamine drug Captagon hidden in a shipment of pomegranates coming from Lebanon. Months later, the wealthy Gulf country extended the ban to all Lebanese products after Lebanon's then- Information Minister George Kordahi publicly criticized Saudi Arabia's war against Iran-backed rebels in Yemen.

item.title: Bathers banned from pitching umbrellas on Italian beach
item.description: Bathers aged between 10 and 65 have been banned from pitching umbrellas on one of Italy’s most beautiful beaches.

item.title: Prince William says AI could help detect homelessness before it happens
item.description: A data lab will be able to analyze and signal warning signs to reduce homelessness.

item.title: Tallest Church in the World Hits Major Milestone on 100th Anniversary of Its Architect’s Death
item.description: The Sagrada Familia in Barcelona has been under construction for over 144 years

item.title: Wednesday’s Mini-Report, 6.10.26
item.description: Today’s edition of quick hits.

item.title: Emotional, creative protests rise up in Mexico City ahead of historic World Cup opener
item.description: Mexicans whose relatives have disappeared, teachers, stadium lifetime ticket owners and more air their grievances ahead of 2026 World Cup opening match.

item.title: Latin America's World Cup Ads Have a Secret U.S. Brands Keep Missing
item.description: Many brands use creative tactics in Latin America that are steeped in local culture, and that many U.S. marketers might find surprising.

item.title: Denied a US visa to watch the World Cup? Have a free TV, says Argentine company
item.description: By Horacio Fernando Soria and Candelaria Grimberg BUENOS AIRES, June 10 (Reuters) - Dozens of Argentines who were denied visas to travel to the United States to see the World Cup will at least have a

item.title: Canada introduces legislation to ban social media for children under 16, regulate AI chatbots
item.description: The Canadian government introduced a new digital safety bill on Wednesday that would ban social media for children under 16 with exemptions for platforms that meet certain safety standards, ‌months after Australia enacted the world's first social media ban for young people. The bill also aims to make ‌AI chatbots safer by setting up a digital regulator to establish safety standards, a government official said.

item.title: Family Starts Fundraiser For 7 Kids Who Went Missing with Pregnant Mom Before She Was Found Slain on Side of Road
item.description: Makala Pendley, 30, was reported missing alongside her children in February

item.title: Israel kills 16 in Lebanon, UN to probe international law violations
item.description: UN investigators to arrive in Lebanon next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties.

item.title: Recent Armenia Elections Will Affect Russia, Iran, US And The World
item.description: The incumbent Armenian leader's election victory will affect Russia, Iran, Turkey, Azerbaijan, Israel and beyond

item.title: No Women? Many People Don’t Even Notice, New Research Finds
item.description: People often notice when a woman is the lone female expert in a group. But do they notice when women are missing entirely? New research suggests the answer is often no.

item.title: Iran 'will stand firm against any pressure or threat,' Pezeshkian warns in reaction to Trump
item.description: Iran "will stand firm against any pressure or threat," Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said. Threats to Iranian infrastructure, including transportation networks, electricity, and water, are “not a show of strength but a sign of desperation,” Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said on X/Twitter.

item.title: Reports: German government will not extend fuel discount
item.description: The German government will not extend fuel discounts beyond the end of June, the tabloid newspaper Bild reported on Wednesday. “After lengthy discussions, we have decided that we will allow the fuel rebate to expire on 30 June as planned,” Sepp Müller, deputy leader of the conservatives' parliamentary group, told the newspaper.

item.title: Pregnant mother found murdered in Mexico as frantic search for missing children ends with surprise discovery
item.description: Mexican authorities arrested the partner of Indiana mother Makala Pendley, who was found dead in Chiapas after disappearing with her seven children.

item.title: WSJ: NKorea Could Boost Uranium Capacity by 75%
item.description: WSJ: NKorea Could Boost Uranium Capacity by 75%

item.title: Indiana mother of 7 who went missing in Mexico is found dead, her family says
item.description: An Indiana woman who had allegedly been missing in Mexico for months with her seven children was found dead, her family said.

item.title: Pope blesses Barcelona’s Gaudi-designed towering architectural masterpiece
item.description: Exactly one hundred years since the architect’s death, Pope Leo XIV visited the iconic basilica to celebrate a Mass and inaugurate the tower which makes the church the tallest in the world.

item.title: Downed Apache Marks at Least 43 U.S. Aircraft Damaged or Destroyed in Iran War
item.description: Two pilots rescued after helicopter was downed near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: World court prosecutor who went after Netanyahu for war crimes suspended over sexual misconduct
item.description: Karim Khan, who sought arrest warrants for Netanyahu, suspended from the International Criminal Court amid allegations of serious misconduct involving nonconsensual sexual activity.

item.title: Air Canada pilot flew for decades without correct license
item.description: The pilot is now facing charges and is no longer flying with Air Canada.

item.title: Albania protests swell as opposition to Kushner resort persists
item.description: By Fatos Bytyci and Edward McAllister TIRANA, June 10 (Reuters) - Thousands took to the streets of Albania's capital Tirana on Wednesday in the largest demonstration yet against the development of a

item.title: India summons US envoy over attack on ship carrying Indian sailors off Oman
item.description: Three Indian seafarers are missing after Oman rescues 21 others following a US attacks on the Palau-flagged Settebello.

item.title: Officials Try To Reassure World Cup Fans That Cartel-Attacked City Is Safe
item.description: A massive security presence is in place before the matches begin on Thursday.

item.title: Israeli Defence Minister Katz: Conflict with Iran is 'far from over'
item.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Wednesday that his country's conflict with Iran is "far from over. " Speaking at an awards ceremony, Katz said: "If Iran attacks Israel, it will suffer a severe blow, just as we did a few days ago.

item.title: US military fires on oil tanker in Gulf of Oman
item.description: The US military said on Wednesday it had attacked a tanker in the Gulf of Oman which was allegedly attempting to transport Iranian oil. The engine room of the Settebello vessel, which was sailing under the Palau flag, was fired upon after the crew defied instructions, the US CENTCOM command responsible for the region wrote on X.

item.title: German Court Holds Google Liable for False AI Overview Claims
item.description: German court ruling makes Google liable for false AI Overview claims, setting precedent that could reshape AI liability across search platforms globally.

item.title: See All of the Best Photos of Pope Leo and the Spanish Royals at the Sagrada Família
item.description: Pope Leo XIV blessed the Sagrada Família’s final tower in Barcelona on Wednesday, joined by King Felipe and Queen Letizia for a historic mass marking the centennial of architect Antoni Gaudí’s death.

item.title: David Briscoe, AP journalist who chronicled Philippines' democratic revolution, dies at 82
item.description: David Briscoe, a journalist for The Associated Press who chronicled the collapse of dictatorship and the rebirth of democracy during a dramatic period of upheaval in the Philippines, has died, his family said. Briscoe died Sunday at an assisted living facility in Kapolei, Hawaii, said his wife, Leonor Briscoe. In a career spanning decades and continents, Briscoe brought a reporter’s curiosity to his native Utah, to Washington and to Hawaii.

item.title: Ebola cases in DRC surpass 600 as officials report struggles with community outreach
item.description: Health officials are following 5,681 contacts, but only 56% have been reached.

item.title: The hunger crisis experts warned about is here – and it’s about to get worse
item.description: Pregnant women in Kabul, sheep-herders outside of Modigushi, the urban-poor in Colombo. As the war in Iran passes 100 days, these are the people on the front line of a new hunger crisis.

item.title: Guadalajara ramps up security and promises safe World Cup after cartel violence scare
item.description: With the World Cup about to begin, heavily armed police and National Guard officers are patrolling the streets of Guadalajara as authorities try to reassure visitors and residents that security won’t be an issue in a city that was rocked by cartel violence earlier this year. Guadalajara, the capital of Jalisco state, will host four World Cup matches including South Korea against Czech Republic on the opening day Thursday. Mexico is co-hosting the tournament with the United States and Canada.

item.title: Ramat David Airbase hit by missile fragment during Sunday's Iran escalation, IDF confirms to 'Post'
item.description: The IDF confirmed to The Jerusalem Post that a missile fragment struck Ramat David Airbase during the recent Iran escalation, causing minor damage but no injuries or disruption to flight operations. Ramat David Airbase in northern Israel was hit by a missile fragment overnight between Sunday and Monday during the escalation between Israel and Iran, the IDF confirmed to The Jerusalem Post on Wednesday.

item.title: Teacher strike keeps 1.4 million Mexican students out of class
item.description: Some 1.4 million students remain out of class in Mexico due to a strike by the National Coordinator of Education Workers on the eve of the World cup.

item.title: Hong Kong files charges over deadliest fire in decades
item.description: Hong Kong filed manslaughter charges against several people and companies on Wednesday over the world's deadliest residential building fire in decades, which killed 168 people at a public housing estate last year.The court heard that those accused of manslaughter "unlawfully" killed 168 from the residential buildings in November, including residents and a firefighter.

item.title: Meet Airbus' Newest Uncrewed Additions
item.description: Manufacturer unveils two new uncrewed aircraft, one based around its H145 helicopter.

item.title: Just 1 in 10 Europeans now view the U.S. as an ally, according to poll
item.description: A growing number of Europeans see the U.S. as a rival or an adversary, especially in Denmark, France, Spain and Switzerland, according to the poll.

item.title: After Belfast Riots, UK Reminds Social Platforms They're Obligated To Remove Hateful Content
item.description: The UK's communications regulator has reminded social media platforms they have a duty to minimize hateful content, not encourage it.

item.title: Violent anti-immigration protests erupt in Belfast after stabbing attack
item.description: Despite calls for calm, masked anti-immigration protesters in Belfast set a bus, vehicles and some homes on fire in what one politician called "a race-based pogrom."

item.title: Referee barred from entering U.S. for World Cup receives a hero’s welcome in Somalia
item.description: Somalia is not competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport Wednesday, you’d think the country had won the trophy.
